hardChecks.label: Citation source ids
hardChecks.expectation: Every cited source id exists in the source packet

hardChecks.label: Cost metadata
hardChecks.expectation: estimatedUsd is present when pricing is configured

hardChecks.label: Token metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Input, cached-input, and output token counts are coherent

hardChecks.label: Provider and model metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Provider, model, and prompt version are present

hardChecks.label: Judge input boundary
hardChecks.expectation: Judge prompt omits eval-only object keys and holdout tuning fields

hardChecks.label: Judge claim coverage
hardChecks.expectation: LLM judge returns exactly one judgment for every briefing claim

claimJudgments.claimText: Lead the recovery message with symptoms seen in partial installs and provide one safe next action, because partial installs created the most confusion about rerunning setup, credentials, and local state.
claimJudgments.explanation: S1 states partial installs were the biggest source of confusion and agents wanted a recovery message that starts with symptoms and gives one safe next action; it also mentions confusion about rerunning setup, credentials, and local state.

claimJudgments.claimText: Do not describe the issue as universally “resolved” unless the environment caveat is prominent; engineering recovery verification is complete for macOS and Linux, while Windows recovery steps are still being validated due to a credential-cache issue.
claimJudgments.explanation: S2 says the word “resolved” was flagged as too broad unless the environment caveat stayed visible; engineering verified recovery on macOS and Linux; Windows recovery steps still being validated and there was an unresolved credential-cache issue.

claimJudgments.claimText: Structure the message to clearly separate: (1) who is affected, (2) what action they should take, and (3) which environments remain pending validation—placing the Windows caveat in the first paragraph.
claimJudgments.explanation: S3 explicitly requests separating who is affected, recommended action, and pending validation, and asks for the Windows caveat in the first paragraph rather than a footnote.

claimJudgments.claimText: Reference the patched CLI’s new recovery command as the primary remediation, since it checks stale credentials and cleans partial install state; note it is currently available in the release candidate.
claimJudgments.explanation: S4 states the patched CLI adds a recovery command that checks stale credentials and cleans partial install state, and that it is available in the release candidate.

claimJudgments.claimText: Align communications with documentation readiness: once the release candidate is promoted, customer communication should not send users to the old installer flow; required doc updates include the recovery command, credential cleanup instructions, and updated screenshots (Windows screenshots are pending).
claimJudgments.explanation: S5 requires docs include the new recovery command, credential cleanup instructions, and screenshots for the patched installer path; notes Windows screenshots pending; and states customer communication should not point users to the old installer flow once the release candidate is promoted.

missingImportantEvidence: Support requested any generated incident briefing match the macro segmentation (verified macOS, verified Linux, pending Windows). (S6)
recommendationJudgment.explanation: The recommendation matches the packet: symptom-led + one safe action (S1), environment caveats and avoiding universal “resolved” (S2/S3), use of the new recovery command (S4), and doc alignment (S5). It also mentions matching Support’s macro segmentation, but does not cite S6 and the evaluation notes it as missing evidence rather than incorrect.

citationSupport.note: S1 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S2 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S3 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S4 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S5 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.

notes: Hybrid evaluator output: deterministic hard checks plus structured LLM judge evidence. Manual spot checks are still required before claiming product improvement.
